summary: Auto-executes AES-decrypted opaque payload via new Function on require
details: On require/import, index.js calls pkg.run() at module top level, which resolves to runDefaultDecodedFunction in src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js. That function AES-256-GCM-decrypts a ~9 KB embedded ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase ("default-dev-passphrase") and salt ("encode-npm-c-salt"), additionally runs the cleartext through decodeCodec and decodePosition from external dependencies, and then passes the resulting code to `new Function("require", runnable)(require)`. Any consumer that imports the package automatically executes the decoded blob with full access to require(). The package ships its own decryption keys, so the multi-layer encoding (position codec + custom codec + AES-GCM) has no benign purpose — it serves only to hide the payload from review while preserving auto-execution. Package metadata corroborates malicious intent: empty author field, generic README, and scripts.start/decode/test all point at a crypto/lib.min.js path that is not present in the tarball, indicating an additional execution surface dropped post-install or in a sibling release.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
